question: Does the image follow a split layout with a mesophyll cell on the left, a flowchart in the middle, and a chemical formula on the right?
Is a microscopic cross-section of a plant mesophyll cell clearly depicted on the left side?
Are the thylakoid membranes and stroma structures visually distinguishable within the chloroplast?
Is the complex chemical molecular structure formula of chlorophyll a depicted on the right side?
Is there a biochemical reaction flowchart in the middle that visually connects the left and right components?
Does the flowchart include multiple arrows and cyclic pathways?
Is the text label 'CO2' (or Carbon Dioxide) clearly visible as a reactant?
Is the text label 'H2O' (or Water) clearly visible as a reactant?
Is the text label 'C6H12O6' (or Glucose) clearly visible as a product?
Is the text label 'O2' (or Oxygen) clearly visible as a product?
